questions.prompt:

 int count = 5; double multiplier = 2.5; int answer = (int)(count * multiplier); answer = (answer * count) % 10; System.out.println(answer);

What is printed as a result of executing the code segment?

questions.prompt:
One of the rules for converting English to Pig Latin states: If a word begins with a consonant, move the consonant to the end of the word and add "ay". Thus "dog" becomes "ogday," and "crisp" becomes "rispcay". Suppose s is a String containing an English word that begins with a consonant. Which of the following creates the correct corresponding word in Pig Latin? Assume the declarations:

 String ayString = "ay"; String pigString;

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method, which is intended to return the greatest even integer that is less than or equal to the product of the method’s two parameters.

 /** Precondition 0.0 <= rating < 1.0; * 0 <= multiplier <= 10 */ public int processRating(double rating, int multiplier) { int intRating = (int)rating * multiplier; if (intRating % 2 == 1) { intRating--; } return intRating; }

Which of the following best describes why the method does not work as intended?

options.content:
A.

The method always returns 0 because the cast to an int is applied incorrectly

B.

The method never returns 0 because the cast to an int is applied incorrectly

C.

The method can return both even and odd values because it identifies odd values incorrectly

D.

The method can return both even and odd values because it fails to correctly convert an odd value to an even value

E.

There is no error. The method correctly returns the greatest even integer that is less than or equal to the product of the method’s two parameters.

int x = 10, y = 0; while (x > 5) { y = 3; while (y < x) { y *= 2; if (y % x == 1) y += x; } x -= 3; } System.out.println(x + " " + y);

What will be output after execution of this code segment?

questions.prompt:
Consider the method reverse:

 /** Precondition: n > 0. * Postcondition: * - Returns n with its digits reversed. * - Example: If n = 234, method reverse returns 432. * @param n a positive integer * @return n with its digits reversed */ int reverse(int n) { int rem, revNum = 0; /* code segment */ return revNum; }

Which of the following replacements for /* code segment */ would cause the method to work as intended?

I

for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++)

{

rem = n % 10;

revNum = revNum * 10 + rem;

n /= 10;

}

II

while (n != 0)

{

rem = n % 10;

revNum = revNum * 10 + rem;

n /= 10;

}

III

for (int i = n; i != 0; i /= 10)

{

rem = i % 10;

revNum = revNum * 10 + rem;

}
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:

E.

Many numbers will be printed. Infinite loop.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method, which is intended to create and return a new string where the characters of each adjacent pair in str are swapped. For example, if the value of str is "abcdef", then the method should return "badcfe".

/** Precondition: The length of str is even. */ public String mixUpString(String str) 
{ int i = 0; String result = ""; while (i <= str.length()) // Line 7
{ result += // Line 9
 str.substring(i + 1, i + 2); // Line 10 result += 
 str.substring(i, i + 1); i = i + 2; // Line 14 } return result; }

Which of the following changes, if any, should be made so that the mixUpString method works as intended?

options.content:
A.

Changing the Boolean expression in line 7 to i < str.length() - 2

B.

Changing the Boolean expression in line 7 to i <= str.length() - 2

C.

Changing the statement that starts on line 9 and ends on line 10 to result += str.substring(i + 2, i + 3);

E.

No change is needed. The method works as intended.

{ private Point center; private double radius; /** Constructs a circle where (a, b) is the center and r is the radius 
 */ public Circle(double a, double b, double r) 
 { // missing code } }

Which of the following replacements for /* missing code */ will correctly implement the Circle constructor?

I center = new Point();
radius = r;

II center = new Point(a, b);
radius = r;

III center = new Point();
center.x = a;
center.y = b;
radius = r;

questions.prompt:
Consider the following class definition:

public class Item 
{ private int id; public Item(int i) 
 { id = 1; } public int nextId() 
 { return id + 1; } public void setId(int i) 
 { id = i; } public int getId() 
 { return id; } }

Assume that Item objects e1 and e2 have been previously declared and initialized. Which of the following code segments will always result in e1 and e2 having the same id value?

I e2.setId(e1.getId());
e1.setId(e2.getId());

II e2.setId(e1.nextId());
e1.setId(e2.nextId());

III e2.setId(e1.nextId());
e1.setId(e2.getId());

int count = 0; for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) if (arr1[i] != 0) 
 { arr1[count] = arr1[i]; count++; } int[] arr2 = new int[count]; for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) arr2[i] = arr1[i];

0, 6, 0, 4, 0, 0, 2

in this order, what will arr2 contain after execution of the code segment?

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method, which is intended to return true if arrays arr1 and arr2 contain the same values in the same order and return false otherwise.

/** Precondition: arr1.length == arr2.length */ public boolean checkArr(int[] arr1, int[] arr2) 
{ int x = 0; while (x < arr1.length) 
 { if (arr1[x] != arr2[x]) 
 { /* missing statement 1 */ } else 
 { x++; }
 } /* missing statement 2 */ }

Which of the following can be used to replace /* missing statement 1 */ and /* missing statement 2 */ so that the method works as intended?

options.content:
A.

/* missing statement 1 */ is return true;
/* missing statement 2 */ is return false;

B.

/* missing statement 1 */ is return false;
/* missing statement 2 */ is return true;

C.

/* missing statement 1 */ is return false;
/* missing statement 2 */ is return false;

D.

/* missing statement 1 */ is x = 0;
/* missing statement 2 */ is return true;

E.

/* missing statement 1 */ is x = 0;
/* missing statement 2 */ is return false;

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method:

public int mystery (int[] data) 
{ int count = 0; /* missing code */ return count; }

Assume data contains the values 2, 15, 7, 9, 12,and 6, in that order. Which of the following code segments can replace /* missing code */ so that when executed the mystery method will return 4?

options.content:
A. 
int num = data.length / 2; for (int i = 0; i < data.length; i++) 
{ if (data[i] % num == 0) 
 { count++; } }

B. 
int num = data.length / 2; for (int i = 0; i < data.length; i++) 
{ if (data[i] % num != 0) 
 { count++; } }

C. 
for (int i = 0; i < data.length; i++) 
{ if (i % data[i] == 0) 
 { count++; } }

D. 
for (int i = 1; i < data.length; i++) 
{ if (data[i] % i == 0) 
 { count++; } }

E. 
for (int i = 1; i < data.length; i++) 
{ if (data[i] % i != 0) 
 { count++; } }

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method, which is intended to return the length of the shortest string contained in wordArr.

/** Precondition: wordArr.length > 0 */ public int getShortestLength(String[] wordArr) 
{ int minLen = 0; // Line 4 for (int j = 0; // Line 6 j < wordArr.length; j++) // Line 7
 { if (wordArr[j].length() < minLen) // Line 9
 { minLen = wordArr[j].length(); // Line 11 } } return minLen; }

Which of the following changes, if any, is required in order for the method to work as intended?

options.content:
A.

No change is required. The method works as intended.

B.

Changing the assignment statement in line 4 to int minLen = wordArr[0].length();

C.

Changing the Boolean expression in line 7 to j < wordArr.length()

D.

Changing the Boolean expression in line 9 to wordArr[j].length() <= minLen

E.

Changing the assignment statement in line 11 to minLen = j;

questions.prompt:
Assume that a square matrix mat is defined by:

int[][] mat = new int[SIZE][SIZE]; // SIZE is an integer constant >= 2

What does the following code segment do?

for (int i = 0; i < SIZE - 1; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < SIZE - i - 1; j++) swap(mat, i, j, SIZE - i - 1, SIZE - j - 1);

You may assume the existence of this swap method:

/** Interchange mat[a][b] and mat[c][d]. */ public void swap(int[][] mat, int a, int b, int c, int d)

options.content:
A.

Reflects mat through its major diagonal. For example,

2 6 2 4

→

4 3 6 3

B.

(B) Reflects mat through its minor diagonal. For example,

2 6 3 6

→

4 3 4 2

C.

(C) Reflects mat through a horizontal line of symmetry. For example,

2 6 4 3

→

4 3 2 6

D.

Reflects mat through a vertical line of symmetry. For example,

2 6 6 2

→

4 3 3 4

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
A matrix (two-dimensional array) is declared as:

int[][] mat = new int[2][3];

Consider the following method:

public static void changeMatrix(int[][] mat) 
{ for (int r = 0; r < mat.length; r++) for (int c = 0; c < mat[r].length; c++) if (r == c) mat[r][c] = Math.abs(mat[r][c]); }

If mat is initialized to be

-1 -2 -6 -2 -4 5

Which matrix will be the result of a call to changeMatrix(mat)?
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method, which is intended to return the total number of consecutive 0s at the end of each row of a two-dimensional array. For example, a row containing the values 1, 0, 2, 0,and 0, in that order, would cause the count to be incremented by 2. If a row of the array contains all 0s, the count is incremented by the number of elements in the row.

public int countZerosAtEnd(int[][] arr) 
{ int count = 0; for (int j = 0; j < arr.length; j++) 
 { boolean stillZero = true; for (int k = arr[0].length; k >= 0; k--) 
 { if (stillZero && arr[j][k] == 0) 
 { count++; } 
 else 
 { stillZero = false; } } } return count; }

The method does not work as intended. Which of the following best describes the error in the method?

options.content:
A.

The method counts all the 0s in each row.

B.

The method counts 0s at the beginning of each row instead of at the end of each row.

C.

The method counts every element in the array because the condition in the if statement is incorrect.

D.

The method does not traverse all rows because the outer for loop header is incorrect.

E.

The method throws an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException because the inner for loop header is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method:

public void printSomething(int[][] arr) 
{ String output = ""; for (int r = 0; r < arr[0].length; r++) 
 { for (int c = 0; c < arr.length; c++) 
 { output += arr[r][c] + " "; } output += "\n"; } System.out.println(output); }

The printSomething method is called with a two-dimensional array of integers with two rows and three columns. The first row of the array contains the values 1, 2, and 3, in that order, and the second row of the array contains the values 4, 5,and 6, in that order. What, if anything, is printed when the method is executed?

options.content:
E.

Nothing is printed because an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown.

questions.prompt:
public class BankAccount 
{ private double balance; public BankAccount() 
 { balance = 0; } public BankAccount(double acctBalance) 
 { balance = acctBalance; } public void deposit(double amount) 
 { balance += amount; } public void withdraw(double amount) 
 { balance -= amount;}
 public double getBalance() 
 { return balance; } } public class SavingsAccount extends BankAccount 
{ private double interestRate; public SavingsAccount()
 { /* implementation not shown */ } public SavingsAccount(double acctBalance, double rate) 
 { /* implementation not shown */ } public void addInterest() 
 { /* Add interest to balance */ } } public class CheckingAccount extends BankAccount 
{ private static final double FEE = 2.0; private static final double MIN_BALANCE = 50.0; public CheckingAccount(double acctBalance) 
 { /* implementation not shown */ } /** FEE of $2 deducted if withdrawal leaves balance less 
 * than MIN_BALANCE. Allows for negative balance. */ public void withdraw(double amount) 
 { /* implementation not shown */ } }

A program to test the BankAccount, SavingsAccount, and CheckingAccount classes has these declarations:

BankAccount b = new BankAccount(1400); BankAccount s = new SavingsAccount(1000, 0.04); BankAccount c = new CheckingAccount(500);

Which method call will cause an error?

private Integer[] a; /** Precondition: a[0], a[1]...a[a.length-1] is an unsorted array * of Integer objects. * Postcondition: Array a is sorted in descending order. */ public void insertionSort() 
{ for (int i = 1; i < a.length; i++) 
 { Integer temp = a[i]; int j = i - 1; while (j >= 0 && temp.compareTo(a[j]) > 0) { a[j + 1] = a[j]; j--; } a[j + 1] = temp; } }

An array of Integer is to be sorted biggest to smallest using the insertionSort method. If the array originally contains:

1 7 9 5 4 12

What will it look like after the third pass of the for loop?

questions.prompt:
Consider the following class definitions:

public class Cow 
{ private int yearOfBirth; private int weightAtBirth; public Cow(int y, int w) 
 { /* implementation not shown */ } public void speak() 
 { System.out.print("Moo"); } } public class Jersey extends Cow 
{ public Jersey(int y, int w) 
 { /* implementation not shown */ } public void speak() 
 { super.speak(); super.speak(); } }

Consider the following code segment, which appears in a class other than Cow or Jersey:

Cow elise = new Jersey(2018, 62); elise.speak();

What, if anything, will be printed as a result of executing the code segment?

options.content:
D.

Nothing is printed. There will be a compile-time error because a Cow variable can only reference a Cow object.

E.

Nothing is printed. There will be a compile-time error because super can only be called from the first line of the subclass method.
